input:
In the desert of Arabia, squads of pursuers were frantically searching for them. The Prophet’s plan was an elaborate one: as soon as he arrived at the cave, he gave the two camels to ʿAbdullāh ibn Arqat, a guide they had hired, and told him to take them to Madinah using an unfamiliar route. Thus the camels were not a burden to them in their three days of hiding in the cave. ʿĀmir ibn Fuhayrah, Abū Bakr’s servant and his shepherd, took his sheep for grazing during the day.
Source: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 14: The Journey to Madinah

output.scene_title: The Prophet's Elaborate Plan in the Desert
output.source_reference: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 14: The Journey to Madinah

characters.name: Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)
characters.appearance: Early 50s, medium build, dignified upright posture. Wearing a simple white thobe with a green cloak draped over shoulders, white turban with tail between shoulder blades. Simple leather sandals. Always shown from behind, face never visible. Distinguished by green cloak and subtle warm light.

characters.name: ʿAbdullāh ibn Arqat
characters.appearance: Late 30s, dark olive skin, athletic build, strong facial features. Thick black beard, neatly trimmed. Wearing simple brown woolen cloak, off-white cotton thobe, worn leather sandals. Practical, rugged appearance.

characters.name: ʿĀmir ibn Fuhayrah
characters.appearance: Mid 20s, light olive skin, lean build, youthful features. Short black beard, neatly kept. Wearing off-white cotton thobe, plain brown woolen cloak, simple leather sandals. Carrying a staff for guiding sheep.

characters.name: Abu Bakr al-Siddiq
characters.appearance: Late 40s, light olive skin, slender build, slightly shorter than average. Thin grey-streaked beard with slight reddish henna tint, neatly kept. Wearing off-white cotton thobe, plain brown woolen cloak, white cloth wrapped as loose head covering. Worn leather sandals. Kind gentle eyes, slightly stooped shoulders.

output.moral_lesson: Planning and preparation can make a big difference in overcoming challenges.
output.narrative_text: In the desert, squads of pursuers were searching for the Prophet and his companions. The Prophet had a clever plan to throw them off their trail: he gave their camels to a guide and told him to take them to Madinah using a different route. Meanwhile, Abu Bakr's servant, ʿĀmir, took his sheep for grazing during the day, keeping them safe from harm.

compliance.notes: No graphic violence, no idols or modern objects shown. Prophet shown from behind, companions shown with full faces.

output.image_prompt: Gouache desert landscape illustration. Distant dark shapes of pursuers on the horizon, sun beating down on sandy dunes. A man in white thobe and green cloak, white turban, seen entirely from behind, standing near a small cave entrance, giving a guide a staff. The guide, wearing brown cloak and off-white thobe, is looking at the camels. Meanwhile, in the distance, a young shepherd with staff and sheep is walking in the opposite direction. No modern objects. Desert sun and dusty atmosphere.
